required_sections.notes: All four required sections present with substance. Learning Objectives: 6 bullets (~90 words). Introduction: ~155 words, strong hook, establishes Python-as-connector framing. Core Concepts: 5 subsections with extensive content. Key Takeaways: 6 bullets (~120 words) mapping directly to stated learning objectives.

citation_density.notes: All 5 primary resources cited at least once. [1] cited extensively throughout every section. [2] cited in section 5.3 (Google Colab bullet), REPL subsection, Implementation step 3, Best Practices, and Hands-On Exercise. [3] cited at three separate points: section 5.3 ('variables [3]'), Implementation ('how to write a variable [3]'), and Hands-On Exercise B ('variables [3]'). [4] cited throughout Core Concepts, Implementation, Real-World Patterns, Best Practices, and Hands-On. [5] cited in Introduction, research assistant pipeline, section 5.5 ecosystem/PyPI subsections, Real-World Patterns example 2, Hands-On Exercise B, and Key Takeaways. Claim citation rate across Core Concepts, Implementation, and Real-World Patterns estimated at ~93% — well above the 60% threshold. Previous cycle hard-fail on [3] is resolved.

sequence_drift.notes: No forward references that violate sequencing. Variables, data types, conditionals, loops named and explicitly deferred to 'topics 11.2 through 11.6' — acceptable mention-and-defer. API technical details deferred to week 12 with pointer. Spec-first discipline deferred to topic 11.7 (sections 5.2 and Implementation). Golden rule deferred to topic 11.8 (section 5.4). Google Colab named as coursework environment without teaching setup (Colab setup is topic 11.2). REPL introduced at a conceptual level as part of Python's interpreted nature — appropriate for this orchestration-role topic. All M1–M4 back-references (flowcharts, pseudocode, HITL, probability, responsible AI, decomposition, abstraction, specification writing) are appropriate.

length_sanity.notes: Estimated word count ~3370 words, within the 3000–4400 standard profile range. Previous cycle failure (word count ~2350, 21.7% below floor) resolved. Expansion was achieved via: added multi-service orchestration example in 5.1, added research assistant pipeline example in 5.1, added syntax errors and REPL subsections in 5.3, expanded section 5.5 with ecosystem-effect and PyPI subsections, added document summarisation pipeline in Real-World Patterns, and added Exercise B in Hands-On.

scope_creep.notes: All three previous cycle failures are resolved. Section 5.2 is now a single sentence defer: 'You will learn the spec-first discipline — describing what you want in plain English before writing any code — in topic 11.7.' No teaching content remains. Section 5.4 is now a single sentence defer: 'You will study the golden rule — never run code you cannot explain line by line — in topic 11.8.' No teaching content remains. Implementation section opens with an explicit defer of the 6-step spec-first workflow to topic 11.7, then describes the broad orchestration development pattern (4 high-level steps) from the perspective of what building a pipeline looks like in practice — this is within scope of the orchestration-layer topic and does not pre-empt topic 11.7's methodology content. Best Practices entries for 11.7 and 11.8 are pointers only, not instructional content.

readability.notes: Readability remains strong. All introduced terms defined on first use: orchestration (music-conductor analogy precedes computing definition), API (spelled out, plain definition, technical details deferred), syntax (defined inline), interpreted (defined inline), standard library (defined inline), packages (defined inline), REPL (spelled out with immediate plain explanation), PyPI (spelled out). Analogies are concrete and appropriate for beginners: translator at a business meeting, music orchestrator, sentence-with-no-verb for syntax error, divide-by-zero for runtime error. No jargon dumps. Active voice and second-person address consistent throughout. Sentence length is appropriate. No hedging phrases detected.

verdict: PASS (all 6 checks). Cycle 2 revision resolves all three previous failures: (1) CITATION — resource [3] realpython.com/python-variables/ is now cited at three points (section 5.3, Implementation, Hands-On Exercise B); (2) LENGTH — corpus is ~3370 words, within the 3000–4400 standard profile range; (3) SCOPE CREEP — sections 5.2 and 5.4 are reduced to one-sentence mention-and-defer form, and the Implementation section defers the 6-step spec-first workflow to topic 11.7 before describing the high-level orchestration pattern. Corpus is ready to advance to HITL #2 human review.
